reviews.review_msg: Could not ask for a funnier, smarter, or more understanding professor! Make sure you do all the reading/translations, and remember that participation matters.

Professor Hussey is one of the best profs I've taken a class with! Even if you're not interested in Medieval English, his passion for the subject is contagious. I found this class difficult but extremely rewarding. Highly recommend!

Truly no one is doing it like Matt. He is the funniest, warmest, most engaging professor I've ever had. He is tough but fair when grading essays, and very kind & accommodating. He loves his students so much and wants nothing but the best for them; he creates such a supportive environment. SFU is lucky to have him.

Extremely disorganized teaching style. While he is highly regarded in his field, his ability to convey material is hindered by frequent, inappropriate remarks that are entirely unrelated. Grading is inconsistent and lacks constructive feedback.

Matt is one of the best profs at SFU, hands down. He's super passionate and engaging, and he really cares about his students. This particular class requires you to really sink your teeth in to do well, but Matt is very encouraging and generous with bonus marks and the like. You will get out of the class what you put into it!
this class is hard, but hes an awesome prof. highly recommend. he is basically a stand up comedian.
Best. Prof. Ever. Genuinely super kind, always wants to help, and he's hilarious. He is so, so good and teaching and such a nice person. If you ever have the chance to take class with him, do it!
Amazing Prof. Very clear about what he wanted and was a straight-forward marker. Loved this class. It was definitely a lot of work but it was a great experience.

Professor Hussey was great! He gave excellent feedback (a paragraph response) to every paper & was clear on what was expected for each assignment. I would definitely recommend going to his office hours as he helps you narrow down your thesis (he likes specific) & gives extra insight on the topic and readings at hand.

I've taken 4 courses with Matt so far, and they have all been amazing. He's one of the best profs I have ever had. Everyone says how tough a marker he is, and that's true but he's so willing to help you, and his feedback is always great. Definitely recommend his classes but for students who are passionate enough and willing to put in the work.

Amazing guy. Super upbeat and fun, great sense of humour, very chill and accommodating if you forgot your homework, need to leave class early, etc. However, this was the hardest class I've ever done. There is homework due almost every class, even on quiz days which are almost every other week. It seems easy at first but the work load is brutal.

Matt is a great prof: passionate, inspirational, genuine nice-guy, knowledgable (and well dressed). He gives fantastic feedback-usually around half a page, typed-but it's worth noting that I've never seen him give a mark higher than an A- on a paper. It's annoying, but his feedback is always valid so I suppose those papers could have been better.

My second time with Matt, and again, the course was just amazing. Intensely knowledgable prof who will make you love, or at least respect Medieval lit.  Course can be intense, but he makes up for it by being hilarious.  If you want to become a better English student, or just enjoy an awesome course, take one with Matt!

Hussey is one of the most passionate medievalists I have ever had the privilege of learning from. Very nice human being. Hard marker - but willing to help you work through your writing demons... his tough marking was a wake up call and I owe a lot of my progress to him. Worth every penny!
If you actually love English as a subject then Matt as a professor is awesome. I see a lot of complaints about him being a tough marker, but honestly he's just fair. I thoroughly enjoy his class.

Matt is the best English prof I've had so far.  His classes are interesting, he is a very fair marker, and he is flexible when it comes to assignments.  I had him for both 300 and 400, and I didn't want the semester to end!  He definately makes coming to class enjoyable, although be prepared to work hard and be on top of your reading!

he expects a lot from you! you have to write five papers and for the final exam you have to make sure you know the main themes for about 100 different poems. he assigns poems but never goes over them in class. your grade completely depends on how mean your TA is.
Includes five papers which are worth 60% of the total mark and the marking is very unfair. I would not recommend this course.
Worst English course.Is the course really an introductory course???
Never take this course if u have any respect for your GPA. I still regret choosing it over ENGL 101.

Never take this course if you are looking to improve your GPA.Also don't take this course if you are interested in poetry. I loved poetry before I took this course, but I hate it now. Matt himself is a TA which is unfair for others. It is not at all an introductory course.       Don't regret afterwards, believe me its the worst it can ever be.
Dont take this class. Matt is a nice guy but the class is terrible. Very boring lectures, lots and lots of writing, hard to understand and overall not enjoyable.
I am so glad to drop this course. Definitely would not recommend.

Boring, extremely heavy courseload, super strict about class rules. No electronic devices, including LAPTOPS allowed in both lecture & tutorial. Mandatory attendance in tutorials although it doesn`t count for marks. LOTS of reading, LOTS of writing... and he is NOT hot -.-

Great english prof, he loved what he was teaching and tries to help his students whenever possible. As a lecturer he is quite boring, going over different types of poems and concepts but i wasn't sure which ones were important. As a TA, he is a very fair marker and super nice. I struggle through english myself and he helped a lot.

As a guy, he's great. He's really accomidating and willing to answer questions and help you out. As a lecturer, terrible. He was boring, and completely un engaging. When you teach an 8:30 am class, you need to have a  bit of spice in your lecture, and there was none. I slept through the first few, so I stopped going. Also, my TA was pathetic.
The class was fair overall...the essays were marked fairly, but lectures were extremely boring and useless as he only read long paragraphs of notes for an hour...

All he does is read from his notebook, teaching hundreds of poem. NO clear focus in the class. Hard to grasp which aspect of poetry he wants you to focus on. No clear direction in the class. In terms of difficulty, it is quite difficult as a freshman course. If this is your thing, then you will enjoy. Otherwise, take something else.

I find all he does is read off of his many pages of paragraphs which makes it really boring(might also have to do with it being an 830am class). It is hard to follow which aspects he wants us to focus on in poetry. Overall, i'm not a fan of the course.

Easily the best teacher I've had in my 4 years at university. He expects a lot from his students, but I personally wouldn't have it any other way. Those looking for an easy A need not apply, but those who can match his passion for all things English won't want the semester to end.

(Both ENGL 300 and 400) Not to be repetitive here, but Matt is amazing. He teaches Medieval/Old English, but his personality blends it nicely with a contemporary flavour. He is extremely helpful, very flexible, and very knowledgeable. Lectures are a pleasure; easily one of the best classes I've ever taken. Expect to be on top of your work/readings.

Matt is absolutely hilarious. He can make medieval lit interesting and fun. You have to go to lectures, but who wouldn't want to? He'll crack you up at least once a class. Plus... girl or guy, you'll have a crush on him by then end of it. It wont be an easy A, but it'll be worth working for.

Hi is the most amazing and entertaining professor i have ever had! i loved going to lecture and listening to him talk! the material tends to be a little dry at times, but he definitely makes it interesting. awesome awesome awesome!

Matt is extremley fun, informative, and interesting.  Well versed and passionate about subject. Absolutely must do readings and homework, and memorize otherwise will not do well in the class. If you study can get an A.

Hussey is just fantastic. Extremely bright, funny and completely engaging. He talks fast and will expect you to do the readings (obviously). Doesn't use overheads so a laptop is useful to follow his fast pace. Well worth the effort, he knows his stuff and you are guaranteed to enjoy his class. He's also suuuuper adorable, which doesn't hurt!
Extremely entertaining; the class was so much fun!
I really liked this class Its a lot easier than expected if you go to lecture you will get an A Matt makes this boring medieval crap kinda funny... but seriously though dont expect it to be riveting

Best english prof Ive had, possibly best overall. Makes lectures about old english actually fun (how many people can do that) and is super nice to his students. Not hard to get an A. Take his classes!

This class is difficult; do not let the other people defy you. Dr. Hussey is kind, comical, and passionate about his topic, however he is very well aware of how knowledgeable he is about medival literature, and how little you are, if you know what I mean. He expects extremely intelligent papers from second years; I think his expectations are too hi
No Comments

Matt is probably the best English prof I've had at SFU. He is very engaging - anyone who can make Old English relevant to the 21st century and comical too - is a genius. He's very knowledgable and helpful, and fun to look at. :)

Old English spoken as a rap -- a great sense of humour, easy on the eyes: this prof is one for your "must try" list! He expects you to work for your grade, and he's not a pushover, but he's reasonable and very encouraging.
